Empires & Puzzles Topaz Hero Review: The Ninja of Perfect Precision

Topaz was once the ultimate answer to the minion meta, turning enemy meat shields into mana for your team. But with paper-thin defense in a world of heavy hitters, can this Holy Ninja still make the cut? We break down her charge mechanics, minion destruction, and whether she's worth the darts in 2026.
Topaz Review: Needles, Ninjas, and Minion Nightmares
Topaz: The Acupuncture Specialist Who Turns Minions Into Batteries

Topaz is a 5-star Legendary Holy hero from the Ninja family, originally released during the Ninja Tower event in early 2022. She operates on the unique "Charge" mana speed mechanic characteristic of her family, allowing players to choose between firing quickly for a surgical strike or holding out for a devastating area-of-effect attack. Her primary claim to fame is her absolute hatred for minions—not only does she destroy them, but she also converts their demise into mana for her entire team. While she doesn't have a costume yet, her base form remains a mechanically interesting tool in specific scenarios. She's essentially an acupuncturist who realized that sometimes, the best way to cure a headache caused by enemy minions is to simply delete them.

Topaz sports a classic ninja aesthetic with a vibrant yellow and purple color scheme that screams "royalty with a side of assassination." She wields lethal-looking needles (or perhaps very aggressive chopsticks) and is adorned with lotus flowers, giving her a deceptively serene appearance. The art captures her "Perfect Precision" title well; she looks like she could pin a fly to a wall from fifty paces without breaking a sweat. It is a clean, sharp design that fits perfectly within the Ninja Tower's eastern-inspired theme.
When Topaz was released, her 821 Attack stat was considered monstrous. In the current landscape of Empires & Puzzles, however, the sands of time have not been kind.
Now considered average to low for a legendary damage dealer. Modern hitters often start at 1000+ before limit breaking.
This is her Achilles' heel. By modern standards, this is paper-thin. A stiff breeze from a modern Construct or Goblin hero is liable to knock her over.
Decent, but not enough to compensate for the low defense.
She is significantly stat-crept. To use her effectively in high-level play today, she absolutely requires Limit Breaking (both levels) and full emblems just to survive long enough to fire.
Topaz's special skill is a masterclass in flexibility.
A quick snipe to remove minions from a single target. Useful for opening a hole in a defense or finishing off a weakened enemy.
Hits three targets. This is often the sweet spot, dealing solid damage and clearing a significant portion of the board's minions.

. Against a heavy minion summoner team (like those led by Hulda, Queen Guowang, or the Christmas family), a well-timed x2 or x3 charge can instantly refill your entire team's mana bars, leading to a cascading victory.
With a level 23 Mana Troop (or combination of troop/emblems/bard bonus), you can shave a tile off these requirements, making her x1 charge fire in just 4 ghosted tiles or a match-3 and some change.
390% to all at x3 charge is still a respectable multiplier, but her base attack stat limits the raw output compared to modern nukers who might do 450%+ to all at Slow speed.
The "small amount" is generally observed to be around

If she destroys 15 minions (a full house of 3 minions per enemy), that's a massive mana injection (roughly 45-75% mana bar) for her whole team. Even destroying 3-5 minions provides a noticeable bump that can be the difference between firing your healer now or dying next turn.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Ninja family relies heavily on not getting hit and countering when they do.
The Ninja family bonus is excellent. Even with just two ninjas (which is a common setup), having a
special skills and attacks gives her much-needed survivability. The
chance is a nice bonus, potentially punishing enemies for touching her.
Topaz does not have inherent passives outside of her family bonus. This is a mark of her age; modern heroes often come with two or three paragraphs of innate resistances and boosts.

Upon release, Topaz was a top-tier solution to the "minion meta" dominated by Bera, Freya, and the Christmas heroes. She was a staple in war attacks and a highly desired hero.
Her stats have fallen behind drastically. While her damage multipliers are good, the base number they multiply is low. Furthermore, the introduction of
complicates things. Topaz deals damage equal to 50% of a Mega Minion's max HP, meaning she cannot one-shot them like she does regular minions.

. She is no longer a general-purpose damage dealer. However, in "Undead Horde" wars or against specific minion-heavy defenses, she remains a potent counter. If you can keep her alive, her mana generation mechanic is timeless and incredibly powerful. She is viable but fragile.
Topaz is a Sorcerer. This class allows her to potentially Delay mana generation on enemies she hits, which synergizes well with her multi-target attacks on x2 and x3 charges.
Focus purely on Attack nodes first, then Defense.

Her damage output drops, making her purely a utility hero for minion clearing.

Be aware that she won't clear the board of Mega Minions instantly, leaving your team exposed to their effects.

If you pull Topaz early, rejoice! She is a fantastic damage dealer for map stages and early raiding. Her flexibility teaches you mana management. Focus on keeping her alive with healers and defense buffers. She will carry you through Season 1 and 2 content easily.

Topaz is a "break in case of minions" tool. She shouldn't be on your main defense team. If you have her maxed, she's a keeper for war depth. However, think twice before committing Alpha Aethers unless you constantly struggle against minion teams and lack modern counters like Ogima or the latest Construct heroes. Her stats are the main bottleneck.
Back in 2022, players panicked thinking Topaz was nerfed to not hit all enemies on x3 charge. As one forum user noted,
"There are no silent nerfs... This was a bug, which is fixed."
It highlights how paranoid players are about their favorite toys breaking!
When released, she was hailed as
"That was insane... she gave so much mana on that triple charge."
It was a time when dealing damage
gaining mana was a revolutionary concept.
"Such lazy art... at least try to make her look different from Garnet."

Topaz is like a vintage sports car. She might not have the raw horsepower (stats) of the modern supercars (latest heroes), but she handles corners (mana charge flexibility) beautifully and looks stylish doing it. In a world overrun by minions, she remains a sharp, reliable needle ready to pop the enemy's balloon. While she may not be the terrifying force she was in 2022, leaving her on the bench when facing a minion-heavy team would be a mistake. She’s definitely worth keeping polished in your garage.
A solid, situational offensive weapon that requires protection but rewards smart play with massive mana swings.
A comprehensive review of Topaz, the Holy Ninja hero in Empires & Puzzles. We analyze her stats, minion-destroying skills, and viability in the current meta.
